meta.phase-name: Shared sections and constrained reuse authoring

intent.item: Build a repeatable revenue-page system for a service business, not a generic page builder.
Favor reuse, speed, and consistency over deep layout freedom.
Let editors scale service, location, and campaign pages by updating shared content once instead of hand-editing every page.

problem.item: The phase-17 reusable model proved the interaction pattern, but code-backed presets alone are not enough for real multi-page content reuse across service, location, and landing-page surfaces.
Without CMS-managed shared sections, the composer will accumulate manual per-page edits and create fast authoring debt as the page count grows.
Deep layout power is tempting but premature: arbitrary nesting and freeform layout would add complexity faster than it adds business value for Grime Time's predictable marketing pages.

goals.goal: Introduce a first-class `shared_sections` CMS model that stores reusable section content, metadata, and lightweight version history.
Make shared-section authoring explicit in the composer with dedicated source editing, insert, detach, and replace flows.
Split insertion into clear buckets for Layouts, Sections, and Shared so reusable content is discoverable without confusing presets and live shared content.
Keep the layout model intentionally shallow and constrained so reuse stays compatible with predictable structure trees and future AI automation.
Support lightweight restore for shared sections and pages without pulling phase 18 into approvals, scheduling, or heavy diff tooling.

requirements.item: Phase 18 shared sections are created from the shared-section library and dedicated shared-section routes rather than by promoting existing page sections.

Linked shared sections update all instances only through an explicit `Edit Source` flow that opens a dedicated shared-section editing route using the same builder shell. No implicit global edits are allowed.
Composer actions in phase 18 must include `Insert shared section`, `Detach from reusable`, `Edit source`, and `Replace with reusable`.
Reusable starter presets and CMS-managed shared sections both exist, but they are distinct concepts: presets are code-backed starters, shared sections are editable CMS content.
Layout remains shallow: section -> row -> column -> block. No arbitrary recursive nesting.
Layout patterns stay fixed and opinionated. Avoid a freeform flexbox or slot-builder UI.

Local overrides on linked shared-section instances are limited to safe placement metadata such as visibility, anchor id, and instance label. Content, structure, styling primitives, and layout composition remain source-owned.
If spacing overrides ever exist, they must be tokenized variants rather than raw spacing controls, and they are not part of v1 scope.
Shared sections should support a preview-image field in the data model, but v1 should not depend on visible preview-image UI or manual preview curation.
Initial shared-section taxonomy should use a small set of navigation categories plus tags for semantics.
Dynamic data bindings remain phase-19 work, except where phase 18 needs a stable contract boundary for future shared-section compatibility.
Publish workflow remains single-editor and lightweight in this phase. No approvals or scheduling.

answer.item: Shared sections are a section-level abstraction, not an arbitrary nested-fragment abstraction.
Phase 18 creates shared sections from the shared-section library and dedicated shared-section routes, not by promoting an existing page section into shared content.
Linked reusable instances are edited globally only via an intentional source-edit path.
`Edit Source` should open on a dedicated shared-section route that reuses the builder shell, rather than keeping page and shared-source editing inside one ambiguous drawer context.
Presets and shared sections both exist: presets are code-backed and not source-editable; shared sections are CMS-managed and editable.
Deep layout power is explicitly deferred.
The allowed depth target is section -> row -> column -> block.
Layouts should use fixed patterns such as hero, two-column, three-column, grid, and CTA band.
Responsive controls stay preset-based rather than exposing custom breakpoint design tooling.
Dynamic bindings are deferred to phase 19, with first priority on before/after jobs, testimonials, services plus pricing, service areas, and FAQs.
The future binding model should stay prewired and opinionated rather than becoming a generic query builder.
Mixed static plus dynamic content inside the same section is a valid future requirement.
Phase 18 remains single-editor.
Add lightweight restore for page versions and shared-section versions.
Do not add scheduling, approvals, visual diffing, or a structural diff viewer in this phase.
Linked shared-section instances may override only safe placement metadata in v1: visibility, anchor id, and instance label.
Raw spacing controls are excluded from v1 because they create reuse drift; a tokenized spacing variant can be considered later if a real need appears.
Content, structure, styling primitives, and layout composition must remain source-owned unless the instance is detached.
Preview images are not required in the v1 shared library UI.
The shared-section data model should still include a preview-image field so the library can adopt previews later without schema churn.
Manual preview curation and screenshot infrastructure are both deferred.
Initial library navigation categories should stay minimal: `Hero`, `Content`, `CTA`, `Social Proof`, `Media`, and `Utility`.
Specific business semantics such as `before-after`, `services`, `faq`, and `trust` should be expressed through tags rather than more top-level categories.

non-goals.item: Do not build Webflow-style arbitrary drag-anywhere layout composition.
Do not add container-in-container-in-container nesting or a generic slot engine.
Do not expand phase 18 into full dynamic data-binding UI.
Do not add publish approvals, multi-user locking, or scheduling.
Do not blur code-backed presets and CMS-managed shared sections into one vague reusable bucket.
Do not allow ad hoc local styling or layout drift on linked instances.
Do not expand the initial library taxonomy into a long list of overlapping business categories.

definition-of-done.item: `shared_sections` exists as a first-class CMS entity with stable structure storage, category metadata, and version baseline.

Editors can create and edit shared sections through dedicated shared-section routes, replace a page section with a shared section, detach a linked section, and explicitly edit the shared source through a dedicated shared-section route.
The insertion UI clearly separates fixed layouts, preset sections, and live shared sections.
Linked shared sections show clear linked-state indicators and instance-impact messaging.
Linked shared-section instances enforce the allowed local-override boundary and do not expose raw local content/styling edits.
The composer enforces the intended shallow layout contract and does not regress into arbitrary nesting.
The shared-section library uses minimal navigation categories plus tags, with preview-image support scaffolded in data but not required in the first UI pass.
Shared-section publishing updates all linked published pages, and draft pages resolve against the latest published shared-section version by default in v1.
Pages and shared sections support lightweight restore behavior appropriate for a single-editor workflow.
Focused integration and browser tests cover shared authoring, propagation, detach, replace, and restore flows.

open-questions.item: None currently blocking. The remaining implementation work is execution detail inside the phase contract rather than unresolved product direction.

tasks.task: Define the CMS-backed shared-section contract: collection schema, versioning baseline, usage tracking, minimal category-plus-tags taxonomy, preview-image scaffolding, and source-vs-instance data boundaries.

Implement first-class composer actions for insert shared section, edit source, detach from reusable, and replace with reusable, using an explicit global-edit confirmation flow and a dedicated shared-section editing route.
Ship a CMS-backed shared-section library and insertion flow grouped into Layouts, Sections, and Shared, with linked badges, instance-impact messaging, and the minimal taxonomy model.
Codify the constrained shallow layout model for reusable sections so composition stays at section, row, column, and block depth without turning into a freeform drag-anywhere builder.
Add lightweight restore flows for shared-section versions and page versions, without introducing approvals, scheduling, or full diff tooling.
Verify shared-section authoring, propagation, detaching, replace flows, constrained layout rules, and lightweight restore behavior with focused integration and browser coverage.

Unify the frontend operator surfaces so the site copilot becomes the single floating shell for chat plus impersonation tools, and the page composer opens as a triggered right-side sheet above the live page instead of consuming shell layout width.
